body:
Title: CRA Form T2017 2025 Summary of Reserves on Dispositions of Capital Property.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2017/t2017-25e.pdf

CRA T2017 2025 summarizes reserves on dispositions of capital property. Part 1 A line 5 adds current QFFP reserve lines 2 to 4, and line 6 subtracts line 5 from prior-year line 1. Part 1 B line 11 adds current QSBCS reserve lines 8 to 10, line 12 subtracts line 11 from prior-year line 7, and line 13 adds lines 6 and 12. Part 1 C line 18 subtracts line 17 from line 16 and line 19 adds line 15 and line 18. Part 1 D line 22 subtracts line 21 from line 20. Part 1 E line 25 subtracts line 24 from line 23. Part 1 F line 28 subtracts line 27 from line 26. Line 29 adds lines 19, 22, 25, and 28. Part 2 line 31 adds line 29 and line 30 and is entered on Schedule 3 line 19200. Negative amounts are shown in brackets where the form states that instruction.

Page 3 explains maximum reserve calculations. For most capital property, the reserve cannot be more than the lesser of capital gain multiplied by amount payable after year divided by proceeds of disposition, and capital gain multiplied by the table percentage. The most-property table uses 80% in the year of disposition, 60% in the first year after disposition, 40% in the second year, 20% in the third year, and zero in the fourth year, encoded as 0.80, 0.60, 0.40, 0.20, and 0. The year indices are year of disposition 0, first year after disposition 1, second year 2, third year 3, and fourth year 4. For family farm or fishing property and small business corporation shares disposed of to a child, or dispositions under a QBT or QCC, the same lesser-of calculation applies with a nine-year table: 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, 50%, 40%, 30%, 20%, 10%, and zero, encoded as 0.90, 0.80, 0.70, 0.60, 0.50, 0.40, 0.30, 0.20, 0.10, and 0. The extended year indices are year of disposition 0, first year 1, second year 2, third year 3, fourth year 4, fifth year 5, sixth year 6, seventh year 7, eighth year 8, and ninth year 9. For gifts of non-qualifying securities, the reserve cannot be more than the capital gain or the eligible amount of the gift. For dispositions before November 13, 1981, the reserve is capital gain multiplied by amount payable after the end of the year divided by proceeds of disposition.
